concerns.text: Task T036b implements the mandatory Spec FR-006 sensitivity sweep, but its dependency on T017 (which ratifies the Plan's *waiver* of FR-006) creates a logical deadlock. The task list attempts to satisfy a Spec requirement while simultaneously depending on a governance task that declares that requirement invalid. This violates the Spec's mandatory FR-006 and SC-003 without a ratified amendment, constituting a silent constraint relaxation or a broken workflow.

Tasks T019 and T022 explicitly state 'Last.fm logic is waived' and 'Last.fm join logic is waived'. While the Plan proposes this waiver, the Spec (FR-001, FR-009) mandates ingestion of Last.fm data. The tasks implement a scope reduction (dropping a mandatory data source) without a ratified Spec Amendment in place. This is a semantic weakening of FR-001/FR-009, violating the Spec's requirement to ingest both datasets.

Task T021 is marked 'FAILED: unspecified' in the provided text. This indicates the implementation of the fuzzy-matching fallback (FR-010) is missing or incomplete. FR-010 is a mandatory requirement for handling missing MusicBrainz IDs to ensure SC-001 (≥90% coverage). A task claiming to implement a mandatory FR but marked as failed/missing is a direct constraint violation.

Task T029 implements exclusion logic for years with >20% missing tags, but the Spec Edge Cases state: 'if > 20% of tracks in a year are missing, a warning is raised'. The task adds an *exclusion* (dropping data) that is not mandated by the Spec (which only requires a warning). This is a silent drift in the acceptance criteria for edge cases, potentially altering the dataset used for regression (FR-005) beyond what the Spec defines.

Tasks T040 and T041 implement a streaming loader for MPD only, explicitly removing hardcoded URLs and failing loudly if fetch fails. While this addresses memory constraints (FR-011), it implicitly drops the Last.fm ingestion path mandated by FR-001. The tasks do not include a mechanism to ingest Last.fm (even if waived later), effectively hard-coding the scope reduction into the architecture before the Spec Amendment is ratified.
